items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit 685f2f019a1387dac852b731ef7b9e4632eeacb0 (PR #58396) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58396 added two significant new public APIs in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` 1.0.0-beta.23: 1. **`PlatformHeaders` static class** тАФ Centralizes all cross-protocol HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). This is a new **public** type visible in the API listing. 2. **`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` extension methods** тАФ Replaced six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId`, `AddAgentServerVersion`, `AddAgentServerLogging`, and their `Use*` counterparts) with a single pair. This is documented as a breaking change in CHANGELOG.md, but the README has no code example showing this Tier 3 standalone setup pattern. ## Gaps Found - `PlatformHeaders` is a public class in the API listing (`api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.net8.0.cs`) but is absent from the README's **Key concepts** section тАФ developers building custom middleware or handlers have no guidance that these constants exist. - The `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair is only mentioned in a single sentence in the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84 of README); there is no code snippet showing how to wire up the Tier 3 standalone setup, making the breaking change from 6 methods to 2 invisible to new readers. - The Getting Started section has no "Standalone / Tier 3 setup" sub-section showing `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` usage alongside the existing `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` (Tier 1/2) example. - No sample test file backing a `PlatformHeaders` or Tier 3 snippet exists under `tests/Snippets/`.
ЁЯУР Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** тАФ Edit 1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the "Health endpoint" subsection), add a new `### PlatformHeaders` subsection: ```markdown ### PlatformHeaders A static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages. Refer to these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings in custom middleware or handlers: | Constant | Header | Direction | |---|---|---| | `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request & response тАФ correlation ID | | `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response тАФ SDK identity | | `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response тАФ resolved session ID | | `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request тАФ user partition key | | `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request тАФ conversation partition key | | `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request тАФ W3C trace context propagation | | `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request тАФ Azure SDK correlation | ``` 2. In the **Getting started** section, add a new `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section **after** the existing `### Start a server (recommended)` sub-section. The new sub-section should explain when to use this pattern and include a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Standalone (Tier 3) setup If you are integrating AgentServer middleware into an existing ASP.NET Core application rather than using `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`, call `AddAgentServerCore()` during service registration and `UseAgentServerCore()` during middleware configuration: ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone // In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app: var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args); builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore(); var app = builder.Build(); app.UseAgentServerCore(); // ... map your protocol routes ... app.Run(); ``` This registers the `x-request-id` response header middleware, the server version (`x-platform-server`) header middleware, and inbound request logging тАФ the same middleware that `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` enables automatically. ``` 3. Update the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84) to reference the new Getting Started sub-section instead of re-explaining the methods inline. **File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`** тАФ Edit Add a new test method backing the `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` snippet: ```csharp [Test] [Explicit("Snippets are compiled to prevent rot but require a running server to execute.")] public void Tier3StandaloneSetup() { #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone // In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app: var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args: []); builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore(); var app = builder.Build(); app.UseAgentServerCore(); // ... map your protocol routes ... app.Run(); #endregion } ``` Also add the required `using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;` import if not already present. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Test file path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs` - Add method `Tier3StandaloneSetup` with `#region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` / `#endregion` - Any test-only assertions must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` 2. **README placeholder:** In `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`, under the new "### Standalone (Tier 3) setup" sub-section, insert: ```` ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone ``` ```` The snippet name is `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` тАФ must match exactly between the test region and the README placeholder. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already has the required top-level sections. The following updates are needed: - **Getting started** тАФ Add `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section *(missing)* - **Key concepts** тАФ Add `### PlatformHeaders` sub-section *(missing)* - All other required sections (Prerequisites, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` тАФ confirm README was updated with the Tier 3 snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` тАФ no public type changes expected; verify API listing is unchanged 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and AddAgentServerCore/UseAgentServerCore Tier 3 pattern in Core README
